patch_instructions:
In data/wins.json, locate the WIN with id="012". In its detail_evidence field, find the paragraph that begins "The dome's own WIN-011 concession is fatal to κ." and ends with the exact sentence "The current WIN-012 framing contradicts the current WIN-011 framing on the dome's own live site today." followed by the separator "

". Immediately after that "

" separator (i.e., before the paragraph beginning "The derivation is also circular: assume the Mohe anomaly is real"), insert exactly: [replacement_html] + "

". Do not modify the preceding WIN-011 concession paragraph. Do not modify the subsequent circular-derivation paragraph or any later paragraph (Yang & Wang timing, monitor.py tautological, Exhibit A forensics, WIN-013/014 retcon). Do not touch detail_verdict_text, detail_claim, detail_extra, tldr_evidence, or tldr_verdict — the TLDRs already flag the simultaneous-confirmation contradiction, and the verdict remains "Self-Contradicted"; adding the dielectric-boundary engagement strengthens the existing argument chain but does not change the kill-shot framing, so no TLDR update is warranted.
word_count_delta: +381 words (detail_evidence grows from ~1306 to ~1687 words; ~8,330 -> ~11,300 chars)
replacement_html: The dielectric-boundary-transition reframing concedes more than it saves. WIN-011 on the dome's current live site (wins.html, accessed 2026-04-21) now proposes a “dielectric boundary transition model” under which “the onset and termination of firmament-plate shading drives the unidirectional induction pulse” at C1/C4 — so the dome’s current mechanism positively predicts a signal at first and last contact rather than at maximum lunar coverage. Credit where due: the Yang & Wang (2002) Mohe record shows valleys concentrated at C1/C4, and any viable mechanism has to accommodate that pattern; the dome has moved in the empirically correct direction by abandoning the peak-at-totality shielding framing that Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) refuted. But the move creates three new problems that tighten rather than loosen the noose on κ. Magnitude mismatch. The live PRED-TIER-2 (predictions.html, accessed 2026-04-21) calls for a −10 to −13 µGal pulse at C1/C4 — 50–100% larger than the −6.5 µGal Mohe datum from which κ = 1.67 was originally computed, at comparable latitude and instrument class. A mechanism that predicts ~11 µGal cannot have been “confirmed” by a ~6.5 µGal measurement. Self-filtering detector. The same live page rescues the 1999/2009 superconducting-gravimeter nulls by asserting that the ECM pipeline “attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release” (WIN-013, accessed 2026-04-21). But C1/C4 is the contact phase, so the pipeline the dome invokes to explain the SG nulls is designed to suppress exactly the signal the dielectric boundary transition model claims to predict. The dome cannot have it both ways: either the pipeline passes C1/C4 pulses (in which case the 1999/2009 nulls refute the model) or it attenuates them (in which case PRED-TIER-2 is unfalsifiable by any Level-3 SG record, past or future). Parameter adoption, not prediction. κ = 1.67 nT/µGal was computed under the 2000 gravitational-shielding interpretation of the Mohe signal — the interpretation Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) ruled out and the dome has now explicitly abandoned. Carrying the same numerical value into the dielectric-boundary-transition regime without re-derivation is parameter retention across an incompatible mechanism change, not a new prediction. A coupling constant derives its physical meaning from the mechanism that links its two sides; swap the mechanism and the number becomes a curve-fit residue of a discarded physics. The dome’s honest epistemic update on WIN-011 thus strengthens, rather than rescues, the WIN-012 self-contradiction.
holes_addressed: ISS-1284 / WIN-012.c5 H2 (major): engages the dome's CURRENT WIN-011 position (dielectric boundary transition model) instead of the abandoned shielding framing. Names the mechanism by its live-site label, credits the empirically correct move (C1/C4 concentration matches Yang & Wang 2002), then lands three distinct problems: (i) the forward-predicted magnitude (PRED-TIER-2 −10 to −13 µGal) is 50-100% larger than the Mohe −6.5 µGal datum that anchors κ; (ii) the dome's pipeline rescue for SG nulls suppresses exactly the contact-phase signal the dielectric model predicts, making the prediction unfalsifiable under the dome's own processing chain; (iii) re-using κ = 1.67 after abandoning the shielding interpretation it was derived under is parameter adoption, not derivation — a coupling constant's physical meaning depends on the mechanism linking its two sides. The dome's honest epistemic update strengthens rather than rescues the WIN-012 self-contradiction.
new_evidence_added: Live-site WIN-011 verbatim quote: 'dielectric boundary transition model' and 'the onset and termination of firmament-plate shading drives the unidirectional induction pulse' (WebFetch wins.html 2026-04-21, independently re-verified this run)
Live-site PRED-TIER-2 verbatim: '10–13 µGal downward pulse at C1/C4 contact phases' with asymmetry ~1.152 (WebFetch predictions.html 2026-04-21, independently re-verified this run)
Live-site PRED-TIER-1 verbatim: '−17 to −21 nT umbra-locked ΔB at ≥3 priority stations' (cross-check for the δB/κ arithmetic that was added to the text by EXP-199/200)
Live-site WIN-013 pipeline rescue verbatim: 'attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release' (used in new paragraph to ground the self-filtering-detector claim in the dome's own words)

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'Parameter adoption across a mechanism change is legitimate if the new mechanism predicts the same coupling ratio. κ being numerically the same under both shielding and dielectric boundary transition is a substantive dome claim, not a curve-fit residue.'
anticipated_objections.response: Then the dome owes a dome-geometry derivation of κ = 1.67 from the dielectric boundary transition mechanism — independent of the two observations (BOU 10.9 nT, Mohe −6.5 µGal) that originally defined it. No such derivation exists on the live site as of 2026-04-21. Without one, the shared numerical value is a coincidence the dome has asserted, not a prediction it has made. The new mechanism must independently yield ~1.67 nT/µGal from dielectric-transition physics; nothing on the live site attempts this.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'The pipeline filter doesn't suppress ALL contact-phase transients — only those below a threshold. PRED-TIER-2 at −10 to −13 µGal is above threshold, so it IS falsifiable by SG data — the 1999/2009 nulls just happened to be below whatever filter threshold was in force.'
anticipated_objections.response: Then the dome owes a specific threshold statement consistent with BOTH the 1999/2009 SG nulls (all below threshold) AND PRED-TIER-2 (above threshold). Membach GWR-C021 and the Chinese SG network have instrument noise floors on the order of 1 nGal — roughly four orders of magnitude below the 10-13 µGal PRED-TIER-2 signal. A filter that passes 10-13 µGal pulses but rejects them at 1999/2009 requires either a post-hoc threshold invented to fit both data sets, or a threshold so ill-defined that 'above threshold' has no operational meaning. Either reading converts the rescue into unfalsifiability by threshold choice.

anticipated_objections.objection: The dome defender says: 'Credit our epistemic update; don't then use it against us. We acknowledged Unnikrishnan 2001 and retreated from shielding — that is the scientific process working.'
anticipated_objections.response: Credit is given in the text: 'Credit where due' and 'moved in the empirically correct direction'. The point is not that updating is dishonest — it is — but that updating mechanism while retaining a numerical parameter derived from the abandoned mechanism is a half-move. Completing the update requires either (a) a dome-native derivation of κ from the dielectric model, or (b) dropping κ from the confirmed-WIN list until one exists. The review's quarrel is with leaving the WIN labeled 'CONFIRMED' after the derivation chain that supported it has been severed.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author has made a genuinely honest epistemic update on WIN-011: acknowledged Unnikrishnan et al. (2001) as refuting the 2000 gravitational-shielding interpretation, relabeled the WIN 'CONFIRMED (Mechanism Contested)', and proposed a replacement mechanism (dielectric boundary transition) that PREDICTS a C1/C4 signal — matching the Yang & Wang (2002) timing pattern. This is the scientific process working: observation forces model revision. The dome has also committed to a specific falsifiable forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2: −10 to −13 µGal at C1/C4 for the 2026-08-12 eclipse). Both moves are substantive improvements in epistemic hygiene over the prior framing.
why_kernel_doesnt_save_claim: The update is structurally incomplete. WIN-012's κ = 1.67 nT/µGal was derived from the shielding interpretation — specifically, from the ratio of the BOU −10.9 nT magnetic anomaly to the Mohe −6.5 µGal gravity anomaly, attributed to lunar mass shielding. When the dome abandoned the shielding mechanism, it kept the numerical value of κ without re-deriving it from the new (dielectric) mechanism. The result is a coupling constant unmoored from any mechanism: a fossil of discarded physics sitting in the confirmed-WIN list. Worse, the new forward prediction (PRED-TIER-2) is 50-100% larger in magnitude than the historical datum anchoring κ, so the dome's own forward prediction is numerically incompatible with the 'confirmation' that defines the coupling constant. And the pipeline rescue that explains the 1999/2009 SG nulls ('Chebychev low-pass attenuates contact-phase transients') is designed to suppress exactly the C1/C4 signal the new mechanism claims to predict. Each of the three problems follows independently from the dome's current live text.
cross_references: WIN-011 — receives credit for its empirical update (C1/C4 concentration); same live-site text supplies the dielectric boundary transition quote
WIN-013 — supplies the pipeline rescue quote ('attenuates contact-phase transients before Level-3 release') and is referenced elsewhere in WIN-012 under 'Internal contradiction with WIN-013/014'

PRED-TIER-1 / PRED-TIER-2 (data/predictions.json, predictions.html) — forward predictions whose magnitudes are used to demonstrate the κ arithmetic inconsistency; PRED-TIER-2 is invoked by name in the new paragraph

WIN-025 (Exhibit A 9-station storm data) — also relies on κ = 1.67; if the κ adoption critique lands here, a parallel issue may need to be filed against WIN-025 (curmudgeon c5 cross_win_consistency noted this but it is out of scope for EXP-201)
unaddressed_holes_note: c5 review lists nine holes. EXP-201 scope per tracker and ISS-1284 is H2 (dielectric boundary transition engagement) only. Other c5 holes are either already addressed in the current text (H3 WIN-013/014 retcon and H7 PRED-TIER-2 numerical inconsistency appear to have been integrated by a prior expansion — the detail_evidence final paragraph now contains 'dome's live WIN-013 now adds the escape clause' and the δB/κ = −10.2 to −12.6 µGal arithmetic) or out of scope (H1 critical E-PRED-C fabrication, H4 '[attenuation of]' bracketed quote, H5 'preliminary report' characterization, H6 'only surviving mechanism' overclaim, H8 raw-text staleness, H9 TLDR currency). The decider should route H1 (critical) to a separate expansion — it is the highest-severity c5 finding and the current text still contains the phantom 'E-PRED-C' label flagged by the curmudgeon. Recommend the decider file ISS-1284-companion or escalate c5 H1 as its own medium-priority EXP.
